REFERENCE: This citation contains multiple significant inconsistencies and is likely inauthentic or fabricated. (1) The DOI (10.1016/S0140-6736(19)30808-6) uses the journal code S0140-6736, which is The Lancet's ISSN, not BMJ's. The prefix 10.1016 with that code firmly places it in The Lancet, not BMJ. (2) BMJ article DOIs typically follow the pattern 10.1136/bmj.xxxxx or similar; this DOI does not match that convention at all. (3) The combination of authors, title, journal, and DOI cannot be reconciled — the DOI points to a different journal entirely from the one cited. (4) The journal, volume, issue, and page details do not correspond to a known, verifiable BMJ article with this title. The overall citation appears to be fabricated or severely distorted.
authors: The listed authors (Gray G, Champion J, Bekker L, Moultrie H, Collie S) cannot be verified as the authors of this claimed article in BMJ, and the DOI provided points to a different journal (The Lancet). The author list may be fabricated or incorrectly attributed.

A similarly titled article 'Clinician engineers — time to act' was published in BMJ by Stephen Etches in 2022, but the details (authors, year, DOI) are entirely different. This title appears to be fabricated or mismodeled after a real article.

journal_volume: Volume 367 is cited for BMJ in 2020. Without a valid DOI or verified article, this volume number cannot be confirmed for the given article and authors.
